notes: stop_word (utility, stuff) + meta_word implied; classic vague-intent exemplar from #579 body
too_short (2 words, below MIN_WORDS=4) — stop_word_present not triggered because 'input' is not a stop-word; rejected by length
single_word AND stop_word; single_word path takes priority and returns immediately
4 words, no stop/meta words, implicit verb encoded in 'is' prefix — passes through correctly
action verb (split) + I/O hint (:// substring) — fully specific
meta_word (general) + too_short (2 words) — dual rejection reasons

input: convert hex pair %XX to single byte
notes: action verb (convert) + I/O specifics (to single byte) — high-quality specific intent
